pitfall: Treating Bristol Stage One/OBC work as implementation approval
why_it_matters: Could mislead decision makers and public about democratic authority
mitigation: Use controlled wording and require future decision-box evidence
next_check: Final order/submission route review

pitfall: Treating WECA/MCA strategy or silence as approval consent objection or no-role evidence
why_it_matters: Could select wrong statutory or funding route
mitigation: Use WECA/MCA classifier and preserve no-role/consent gap
next_check: Current-law and funding-dependency review

pitfall: Treating generic DfT guidance as WPL-specific confirmation process
why_it_matters: Could create deficient statutory submission planning
mitigation: Classify DfT engagement and require WPL-specific response or source
next_check: DfT engagement evidence review

pitfall: Selecting a boundary before authoritative GIS and inventory evidence
why_it_matters: Would undermine revenue appraisal EqIA displacement and scheme schedule
mitigation: Require source manifest topology QA and legal/GIS reconciliation
next_check: Stage 4 spatial gate

pitfall: Missing residential spillover CPZ or RPZ mitigation
why_it_matters: Could shift parking pressure to residential streets and create consultation risk
mitigation: Add displacement baseline mitigation triggers and monitoring
next_check: Boundary option appraisal

pitfall: Copying Nottingham charge level elasticity or impact assumptions to Bristol
why_it_matters: Would create unsupported BCR VFM revenue and behavioural claims
mitigation: Require Bristol-specific evidence and transferability review
next_check: Nottingham transferability QA

pitfall: Counting gross levy receipts as economic benefits
why_it_matters: Would misstate Green Book/TAG value for money
mitigation: Classify receipts as transfers and separate resource costs/benefits
next_check: Economic case QA

pitfall: Assembling OBC before section evidence dependencies exist
why_it_matters: Could create false officer or cabinet readiness
mitigation: Keep OBC assembly blocked until dependencies are populated
next_check: Stage 7 OBC gate

pitfall: Launching consultation without authority privacy accessibility and analysis controls
why_it_matters: Creates public-law data-protection and accessibility risks
mitigation: Use launch-readiness register and consultation QA validator
next_check: Stage 8 launch gate

pitfall: Treating agent sign-offs as real public-body approval
why_it_matters: Could mislead real-world adoption and assurance
mitigation: Replace simulation sign-offs with human professional and authority approvals
next_check: Real-world adoption review

pitfall: Treating OBC assurance controls as a passed OBC gate
why_it_matters: Could create false cabinet or officer confidence and premature consultation pressure
mitigation: Require validator gate failure while open blockers remain and ban proceed recommendation wording
next_check: Stage 7 OBC gate

pitfall: Treating statutory dossier controls as a ready statutory submission
why_it_matters: Could create false DfT or Secretary of State readiness and defective legal process
mitigation: Require statutory QA gate failure while open blockers remain and ban submission readiness wording
next_check: Stage 10 statutory submission gate

pitfall: Treating final gate controls as FBC approval statutory submission or implementation recommendation
why_it_matters: Could create false democratic legal financial and implementation readiness
mitigation: Require FBC/statutory gate validator failure while open blockers remain and ban submit/implement wording
next_check: Stage 11 FBC/statutory gate

pitfall: Treating public repository visibility as endorsement approval or professional sign-off
why_it_matters: Could mislead public readers officers members or future agents about the legal and democratic status of the simulation
mitigation: Require public-release QA and keep public visibility is not approval wording visible in README and public/officer packs
next_check: Stage 12A public release gate

pitfall: Treating the critical path or 90-day plan as an approved programme
why_it_matters: Could create false authority to spend procure consult mobilise or claim gates have passed
mitigation: Require handover QA and visible critical path is not approval wording before any handover or next-step claim
next_check: Stage 13A handover gate

pitfall: Treating source notes as verified claim evidence or complete evidence review
why_it_matters: Could create false confidence in OBC/FBC drafting and obscure unsupported source claims
mitigation: Require source-note QA and claim-level evidence review before source-heavy drafting reliance
next_check: Stage 14A source-note gate

pitfall: Treating public-source PDFs as automatically safe for a public GitHub repository.
why_it_matters: Public documents can contain embed identifiers, token-like payloads or personal data that create hosted scanner alerts or disclosure risk when mirrored into Git history.
mitigation: Run secrets-qa before release, omit or redact public sources that collide with token detectors, keep source URLs and hashes in registers, and require explicit approval before any history rewrite.
next_check: Before every public push or source acquisition refresh

pitfall: Treating a current-tree cleanup or dry-run rewrite as live history remediation.
why_it_matters: GitGuardian and GitHub secret scanning can continue to see historical blobs; a force push also affects collaborators and does not clean forks or local clones.
mitigation: Stage 14D completed the live rewrite with force-with-lease and post-push verification; continue to avoid claiming hosted alert closure or fork/cache cleanup.
next_check: Before any history rewrite or force push

pitfall: Claiming a force-pushed clean main history proves GitGuardian closure or external token safety.
why_it_matters: Hosted scanners, forks, caches, old clones and real external token lifecycle controls sit outside the rewritten repository history.
mitigation: State only that reachable GitHub main history passed post-rewrite scans; check GitGuardian directly and rotate any genuine token outside git.
next_check: After GitGuardian alert status is reviewed

pitfall: Using GitHub repo scans or disabled GitHub secret scanning response as proof that GitGuardian closed its alert.
why_it_matters: GitGuardian and GitHub secret scanning are separate systems with separate alert state, cache timing and disposition workflows.
mitigation: Claim only repository-side verification until GitGuardian disposition is checked directly and recorded without token values.
next_check: After GitGuardian alert status is reviewed

pitfall: Treating a legal/governance source-note expansion as legal clearance or complete evidence due diligence
why_it_matters: A large source-note cohort can look authoritative even though it is only a claim-control layer and not a legal opinion, OBC/FBC evidence packet or statutory submission dossier.
mitigation: Use source notes only to control source-use boundaries; require source IDs, line/page references and specialist simulated review before claim reliance; keep backlog gates open.
next_check: Before any legal, OBC, FBC, consultation, public-summary or statutory-dossier drafting task

pitfall: Treating acquired priority source-note coverage as verified claim evidence
why_it_matters: Coverage completion can sound like evidence completion, but source notes only control source-use limits and do not prove claims or replace reviewer judgement.
mitigation: Use acquired-priority source-note coverage complete only as a coverage claim; require claim-level source summaries and reviewer status before drafting reliance.
next_check: Before claim-level source summary or OBC/FBC drafting stage

pitfall: Treating a claim summary as proof of the claim or a substitute for source review
why_it_matters: A tidy summary can look authoritative even when it only records the current simulated review status and limitations.
mitigation: Require underlying source IDs source locations reviewer status limitations and no-go wording before any drafting reliance.
next_check: Before any OBC FBC consultation statutory public or officer drafting stage

pitfall: Treating editable folders templates or scaffolds as assembled decision papers
why_it_matters: Folder and template presence can create false confidence that an OBC FBC statutory submission consultation pack or officer-review paper exists.
mitigation: Require output-specific status rows fail-closed assembly scripts no authored PDF scans and visible no-go wording before any authoring claim.
next_check: Before any document assembly DOCX HTML publication or officer-pack task

pitfall: Treating Nottingham lessons as Bristol forecasts or ready CPZ/RPZ mitigation
why_it_matters: Could mislead cabinet officers or public about displacement revenue congestion mode shift employer behaviour public acceptability or mitigation readiness
mitigation: Use transferability questions no-transfer notes blocked cross-register statuses and public/officer no-overclaim wording
next_check: Before any Nottingham comparator OBC FBC consultation or public drafting

pitfall: Treating green controls polished guides or visual maps as WPL readiness
why_it_matters: Could create false confidence among public cabinet officers or legal teams that approval consultation OBC FBC statutory submission or professional assurance exists
mitigation: Use RAG legend control-stage taxonomy risk-control crosswalk simulation sign-off limits and comprehension validator
next_check: Before any public cabinet officer pack or repository navigation update

pitfall: Treating static Mermaid and Markdown visual checks as accessibility certification or user-tested comprehension
why_it_matters: Could overstate the quality of public/cabinet communication and mask screen-reader colour-blind low-vision keyboard or rendered-visual issues
mitigation: Use no-go captions legends text fallbacks static-source-only register status and EG-0047 open evidence gap
next_check: Before any rendered visual publication or accessibility claim

pitfall: Treating repo-local link checks as proof that external sources evidence or communication are correct
why_it_matters: Could overstate navigation QA and mask external URL liveness content accuracy source sufficiency or user comprehension gaps
mitigation: Use validator output scope caveat and preserve external-link content-truth and readiness no-go wording
next_check: Before any public/officer navigation release or external-link claim

pitfall: Treating reachable URLs or redirects as proof that sources are current authoritative complete or decision-grade
why_it_matters: Could create false assurance for OBC FBC consultation statutory or legal claims without content review source-note refresh or professional review
mitigation: Use reachable not live wording metadata-only pass message redirect visibility and EG-0050 residual gap
next_check: Before any source-refresh current-law evidence-accuracy or drafting-reliance claim

pitfall: Treating resolved register IDs and repo paths as substantive assurance
why_it_matters: Could mask incorrect row wording weak evidence unsupported mitigations wrong risk ratings or false readiness claims
mitigation: Use linkage-only wording primary-key uniqueness checks and EG-0051 residual gap for row substance review
next_check: Before any register assurance dashboard update or decision-grade reliance claim

pitfall: Treating a consistent public blocker row as an exhaustive or adequate risk dashboard
why_it_matters: Could hide material blockers not listed in the README or imply risk ratings mitigations and readiness conclusions are settled
mitigation: Use blocker-surfacing-only wording and preserve separate risk adequacy evidence truth and professional-review gaps
next_check: Before any public/officer status dashboard or blocker-summary update

pitfall: Treating gate-report command references as proof that commands were run or that gate decisions are substantively correct
why_it_matters: Could create false audit confidence and mask stale validation evidence weak judgement or readiness overclaim
mitigation: Use report-structure-only wording and require fresh command execution before completion claims
next_check: Before any gate report acceptance or workflow-completion claim

pitfall: Treating validation evidence logs as professional assurance or readiness evidence
why_it_matters: Could create false confidence that command output proves source truth legal correctness blocker completeness or WPL readiness
mitigation: Use validation-log-only wording and require separate substantive review before reliance
next_check: Before any validation evidence reliance claim
